page_content:
ECON709 Advanced Topics in Applied and Theoretical Macroeconomics (2 Credits) Read, discuss, and analyze current topics in macroeconomics, including asset pricing models, models of economic growth, investment, and the labor market. Speciﬁc topics covered will change from semester to semester depending on the students' and faculty's interests. Intended primarily for students beginning thesis research in economics. Prerequisite: Must have completed a one-year graduate sequence in one of the macroeconomic ﬁelds. Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs.

ECON721 Econometrics III (3 Credits) Oriented towards macro-econometric methods. Topics covered will be selected from the following: Further discussion of topics covered in ECON624, non-stationary time series models (models with deterministic trends, unit roots, co-integration, error correction models, vector autoregressive models), econometric models of conditional heteroskedasticity (ARCH and GARCH models), Bayesian econometrics and methods for Bayesian computation, large-dimensional factor analysis, models of changes in regime, and methods for time series forecasting. Prerequisite: ECON624; or permission of BSOS-Economics department.
